title: SARS May Impact Global Electronics Industry
author: Russ Craig; Peter Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen Group hot topic report warning that the 2003 SARS outbreak could cause major disruptions to the global electronics supply chain concentrated in Asia Pacific. The report documents that Asia Pacific consumed 37% ($52B) of 2002 semiconductor production, with 85%+ of WiFi gear made in Taiwan and PRC. It catalogs facility closures (Motorola Singapore), travel bans (Compal), and corporate shutdowns (CTIC) already occurring, and warns that a PRC quarantine would mean nuclear winter for the semiconductor industry. The analysis identifies just-in-time manufacturing as inherently vulnerable to pandemic disruption and notes critical single-source dependencies on PRC components.
importance_rationale: Prescient early warning about pandemic risk to electronics supply chains; discussed on NBC Nightly News within 24 hours of publication
relevance_rationale: COVID-19 pandemic validated every concern raised; supply chain resilience and geographic diversification remain top industry priorities
prescience_rationale: Predicted supply chain disruptions from pandemic proved dramatically correct during COVID-19; warnings about PRC government transparency repeated in 2020
